)]}'

6509
[["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to \"]],[52,69,[\"Jupyter notebooks\",[true]]],[69,71,[\" (\"]],[71,77,[\".ipynb\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[77,192,[\" files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code.\"]]]]],[192,267,[[[192,267,[\"Claude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\"]]]]],[267,464,[[[267,295,[\"Interaction and Exploration:\",[true]]],[295,312,[\" Claude Code can \"]],[312,326,[\"read and write\",[true]]],[326,363,[\" Jupyter notebooks and is capable of \"]],[363,383,[\"interpreting outputs\",[true]]],[383,464,[\", including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly.\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"•\",1,1,0]]],[464,745,[[[464,485,[\"Aesthetic Refinement:\",[true]]],[485,529,[\" Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make \"]],[529,551,[\"aesthetic improvements\",[true]]],[551,745,[\" to a notebook before sharing it with others. Use Claude to work with Jupyter notebooks\"]]],[null,6]]],[15725,16071,[[[15725,16071,[\"Researchers and data scientists at Anthropic use Claude Code to read and write Jupyter notebooks. Claude can interpret outputs, including images, providing a fast way to explore and interact with data. There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. Specifically telling it to make the notebook or its data visualizations “aesthetically pleasing” tends to help remind it that it’s optimizing for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"6ad4fced-a87f-42bc-a11f-b92cc700fa5f\"],\"c974f500-e788-4321-979e-0256bf205616\"]],[\"68110562-0575-4c7e-b925-bfe3a049f714\"]]]],1]],[[null,null,0.6795124788671918,[[null,15588,16370]],[[[15588,15681,[[[15588,15681,[\"This eliminates the need to remember  gh  command line syntax while automating routine tasks.\"]]],[null,1]]],[15681,15725,[[[15681,15725,[\"h. Use Claude to work with Jupyter notebooks\"]]],[null,6]]],[15725,16071,[[[15725,16071,[\"Researchers and data scientists at Anthropic use Claude Code to read and write Jupyter notebooks. Claude can interpret outputs, including images, providing a fast way to explore and interact with data. There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. Specifically telling it to make the notebook or its data visualizations “aesthetically pleasing” tends to help remind it that it’s optimizing for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"6ad4fced-a87f-42bc-a11f-b92cc700fa5f\"],\"c974f500-e788-4321-979e-0256bf205616\"]],[\"68110562-0575-4c7e-b925-bfe3a049f714\"]]],[[[null,78,191],[0]],[[null,383,463],[0]],[[null,552,595],[0]],[[null,595,744],[0]]],null,false]"]]

[["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to \"]],[52,69,[\"Jupyter notebooks\",[true]]],[69,71,[\" (\"]],[71,77,[\".ipynb\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[77,192,[\" files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code.\"]]]]],[192,267,[[[192,267,[\"Claude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\"]]]]],[267,464,[[[267,295,[\"Interaction and Exploration:\",[true]]],[295,312,[\" Claude Code can \"]],[312,326,[\"read and write\",[true]]],[326,363,[\" Jupyter notebooks and is capable of \"]],[363,383,[\"interpreting outputs\",[true]]],[383,464,[\", including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly.\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"•\",1,1,0]]],[464,745,[[[464,485,[\"Aesthetic Refinement:\",[true]]],[485,529,[\" Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make \"]],[529,551,[\"aesthetic improvements\",[true]]],[551,745,[\" to a notebook before sharing it with others. [["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have 7973
[["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have shifted from using \\\"throwaway\\\" Jupyter notebooks to using Claude Code to build **permanent React dashboards** that can be reused for ongoing\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to \"]],[52,69,[\"Jupyter notebooks\",[true]]],[69,71,[\" (\"]],[71,77,[\".ipynb\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[77,192,[\" files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code.\"]]]]],[192,267,[[[192,267,[\"Claude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\"]]]]],[267,464,[[[267,295,[\"Interaction and Exploration:\",[true]]],[295,312,[\" Claude Code can \"]],[312,326,[\"read and write\",[true]]],[326,363,[\" Jupyter notebooks and is capable of \"]],[363,383,[\"interpreting outputs\",[true]]],[383,464,[\", including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data Use Claude to work with Jupyter notebooks\"]]],[null,6]]],[15725,16071,[[[15725,16071,[\"Researchers and data scientists at Anthropic use Claude Code to read and write Jupyter notebooks. Claude can interpret outputs, including images, providing a fast way to explore and interact with data. There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. Specifically telling it to make the notebook or its data visualizations “aesthetically pleasing” tends to help remind it that it’s optimizing for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"6ad4fced-a87f-42bc-a11f-b92cc700fa5f\"],\"c974f500-e788-4321-979e-0256bf205616\"]],[\"cff927c2-4350-4a92-aee2-84c8682801f0\"]]]],1]],[[null,null,0.6795124788671918,[[null,15588,16370]],[[[15588,15681,[[[15588,15681,[\"This eliminates the need to remember  gh  command line syntax while automating routine tasks.\"]]],[null,1]]],[15681,15725,[[[15681,15725,[\"h. Claude can interpret outputs, including images, providing a fast way to explore and interact with data. There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. [["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have shifted from using \\\"throwaway\\\" Jupyter notebooks to using Claude Code to build **permanent React dashboards** that can be reused for ongoing model evaluations and analytics [2, 3].\\n\\nIn essence, a Jupyter notebook in this context is a **collabor\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the [["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have shifted from using \\\"throwaway\\\" Jupyter notebooks to using Claude Code to build **permanent React dashboards** that can be reused for ongoing model evaluations and analytics [2, 3].\\n\\nIn essence, a Jupyter notebook in this context is a **collaborative workspace for data analysis** that Claude Code can help manage, document, and improve through natural language commands [1]\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to \"]],[52,69,[\"Jupyter notebooks\",[true]]],[69,71,[\" (\"]],[71,77,[\".ipynb\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[77,192,[\" files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code.\"]]]]],[192,267,[[[192,267,[\"Claude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\"]]]]],[267,464,[[[267,295,[\"Interaction and Exploration:\",[true]]],[295,312,[\" Claude Code can \"]],[312,326,[\"read and write\",[true]]],[326,363,[\" Jupyter notebooks and is capable of \"]],[363,383,[\"interpreting outputs\",[true]]],[383,464,[\", including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly.\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"•\",1,1,0]]],[464,745,[[[464,485,[\"Aesthetic Refinement:\",[true]]],[485,529,[\" Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make \"]],[529,551,[\"aesthetic improvements\",[true]]],[551,745,[\" to a notebook before sharing it with others. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing eliminates the need to remember  gh  command line syntax while automating routine tasks.\"]]],[null,1]]],[15681,15725,[[[15681,15725,[\"h. Use Claude to work with Jupyter notebooks\"]]],[null,6]]],[15725,16071,[[[15725,16071,[\"Researchers and data scientists at Anthropic use Claude Code to read and write Jupyter notebooks. Claude can interpret outputs, including images, providing a fast way to explore and interact with data. There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. Specifically telling it to make the notebook or its data visualizations “aesthetically pleasing” tends to help remind it that it’s optimizing for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"6ad4fced-a87f-42bc-a11f-b92cc700fa5f\"],\"c974f500-e788-4321-979e-0256bf205616\"]],[\"0cad3c2e-27b8-45b2-a8fc-67c98fcbe57a\"]]],[[\"286eda9f-c019-47fa-981e-b76ac253f6f1\"],[null,null,0.6624827946303096,[[null,21021,21557]],[[[21021,21089,[[[21021,21089,[\"that’s too complex for editor macros but not large enough for major \"]]]]],[21089,21161,[[[21089,21161,[\"development effort, they use Claude Code like a “slot machine” - commit \"]]]]],[21161,21230,[[[21161,21230,[\"their state, let Claude work autonomously for 30 minutes, and either \"]]]]],[21230,21288,[[[21230,21288,[\"accept the solution or restart fresh if it doesn’t work.\\n \"]]]]],[21288,21356,[[[21288,21356,[\"Creating persistent analytics tools instead of throwaway notebooks\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21356,21426,[[[21356,21426,[\"Instead of building one-off Jupyter notebooks that get discarded, the \"]]]]],[21426,21491,[[[21426,21491,[\"team now has Claude build permanent React dashboards that can be \"]]]]],[21491,21557,[[[21491,21557,[\"reused across future model evaluations. This is important because \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"286eda9f-c019-47fa-981e-b76ac253f6f1\"]]],[[\"29eefec6-82f7-4b62-b525-849dea8b6433\"],[null,null,0.6728679597820794,[[null,21557,22083]],[[[21557,21629,[[[21557,21629,[\"understanding Claude’s performance is “one of the most important things \"]]]]],[21629,21695,[[[21629,21695,[\"for the team” - they need to understand how models perform during \"]]]]],[21695,21769,[[[21695,21769,[\"training and evaluations, which “is actually non-trivial and simple tools \"]]]]],[21769,21836,[[[21769,21836,[\"can’t get too much signal from looking at a single number go up.”\\n \"]]]]],[21836,21869,[[[21836,21869,[\"Zero-dependency task delegation\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21869,21942,[[[21869,21942,[\"For tasks in completely unfamiliar codebases or languages, they delegate \"]]]]],[21942,22013,[[[21942,22013,[\"entire implementation to Claude Code, leveraging its ability to gather \"]]]]],[22013,22083,[[[22013,22083,[\"context from the monorepo and execute tasks without their involvement \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"29eefec6-82f7-4b62-b525-849dea8b6433\"]]]],1]],[[null,null,0.6795124788671918,[[null,15588,16370]],[[[15588,15681,[[[15588,15681,[\"This eliminates the need to remember  gh  command line syntax while automating routine tasks.\"]]],[null,1]]],[15681,15725,[[[15681,15725,[\"h. Use Claude to work with Jupyter notebooks\"]]],[null,6]]],[15725,16071,[[[15725,16071,[\"Researchers and data scientists at Anthropic use Claude Code to read and write Jupyter notebooks. Claude can interpret outputs, including images, providing a fast way to explore and interact with data. 15029
[["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have shifted from using \\\"throwaway\\\" Jupyter notebooks to using Claude Code to build **permanent React dashboards** that can be reused for ongoing model evaluations and analytics [2, 3].\\n\\nIn essence, a Jupyter notebook in this context is a **collaborative workspace for data analysis** that Claude Code can help manage, document, and improve through natural language commands [1, 4].\\n\\nTo think of it another way: if your codebase is the engine of a car, a Jupyter notebook is\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to By understanding the entire codebase, Claude Code helps simplify workflows, making it a powerful tool for software development.\"]]],[null,1]]],[1408,1575,[[[1408,1575,[\"In this tutorial, I’ll explain how to use Claude Code to improve software development workflows by refactoring, documenting, and debugging code. Specifically, we will:\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"62969650-0dff-481c-bba3-8d5fe63e9b31\"],\"cae7a337-fdf0-4a29-9afe-cea19ba0446f\"]],[\"a23a569d-9d33-4d9a-9d26-2db166404471\"]]]],1]],[[null,null,0.6795124788671918,[[null,15588,16370]],[[[15588,15681,[[[15588,15681,[\"This eliminates the need to remember  gh  command line syntax while automating routine tasks.\"]]],[null,1]]],[15681,15725,[[[15681,15725,[\"h. Use Claude to work with Jupyter notebooks\"]]],[null,6]]],[15725,16071,[[[15725,16071,[\"Researchers and data scientists at Anthropic use Claude Code to read and write Jupyter notebooks. Claude can interpret outputs, including images, providing a fast way to explore and interact with data. There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. Specifically telling it to make the notebook or its data visualizations “aesthetically pleasing” tends to help remind it that it’s optimizing for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"6ad4fced-a87f-42bc-a11f-b92cc700fa5f\"],\"c974f500-e788-4321-979e-0256bf205616\"]],[\"d5bdf595-4bb5-446e-9948-bdd53edfcbff\"]],[null,null,0.6624827946303096,[[null,21021,21557]],[[[21021,21089,[[[21021,21089,[\"that’s too complex for editor macros but not large enough for major \"]]]]],[21089,21161,[[[21089,21161,[\"development effort, they use Claude Code like a “slot machine” - commit \"]]]]],[21161,21230,[[[21161,21230,[\"their state, let Claude work autonomously for 30 minutes, and either \"]]]]],[21230,21288,[[[21230,21288,[\"accept the solution or restart fresh if it doesn’t work.\\n \"]]]]],[21288,21356,[[[21288,21356,[\"Creating persistent analytics tools instead of throwaway notebooks\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21356,21426,[[[21356,21426,[\"Instead of building one-off Jupyter notebooks that get discarded, the \"]]]]],[21426,21491,[[[21426,21491,[\"team now has Claude build permanent React dashboards that can be \"]]]]],[21491,21557,[[[21491,21557,[\"reused across future model evaluations. This is important because \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"ed41c3e6-d374-40bb-9d4b-8e6661e09e50\"]],[null,null,0.6728679597820794,[[null,21557,22083]],[[[21557,21629,[[[21557,21629,[\"understanding Claude’s performance is “one of the most important things \"]]]]],[21629,21695,[[[21629,21695,[\"for the team” - they need to understand how models perform during \"]]]]],[21695,21769,[[[21695,21769,[\"training and evaluations, which “is actually non-trivial and simple tools \"]]]]],[21769,21836,[[[21769,21836,[\"can’t get too much signal from looking at a single number go up.”\\n \"]]]]],[21836,21869,[[[21836,21869,[\"Zero-dependency task delegation\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21869,21942,[[[21869,21942,[\"For tasks in completely unfamiliar codebases or languages, they delegate \"]]]]],[21942,22013,[[[21942,22013,[\"entire implementation to Claude Code, leveraging its ability to gather \"]]]]],[22013,22083,[[[22013,22083,[\"context from the monorepo and execute tasks without their involvement \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"bed3dc95-4c91-4600-86d5-0e5ba81f75ea\"]],[null,null,0.6614029868224456,[[null,1033,1575]],[[[1033,1408,[[[1033,1097,[\"Get your team access to the full DataCamp for business platform.\",[null,null,null,\"\"]]],[1097,1408,[\"Claude Code  is an agentic coding tool developed by Anthropic that operates directly in the terminal, assisting developers in refactoring, documenting, and debugging code efficiently. By understanding the entire codebase, Claude Code helps simplify workflows, making it a powerful tool for software development.\"]]],[null,1]]],[1408,1575,[[[1408,1575,[\"In this tutorial, I’ll explain how to use Claude Code to improve software development workflows by refactoring, documenting, and debugging code. Specifically, we will:\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"62969650-0dff-481c-bba3-8d5fe63e9b31\"],\"cae7a337-fdf0-4a29-9afe-cea19ba0446f\"]],[\"a23a569d-9d33-4d9a-9d26-2db166404471\"]]],[[[null,78,191],[0]],[[null,383,463],[0]],[[null,552,595],[0]],[[null,595,744],[0]],[[null,848,870],[0]],[[null,1063,1125],[1,2]],[[null,1220,1309],[0,3]]],null,false]"]]
15394
[["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have shifted from using \\\"throwaway\\\" Jupyter notebooks to using Claude Code to build **permanent React dashboards** that can be reused for ongoing model evaluations and analytics [2, 3].\\n\\nIn essence, a Jupyter notebook in this context is a **collaborative workspace for data analysis** that Claude Code can help manage, document, and improve through natural language commands [1, 4].\\n\\nTo think of it another way: if your codebase is the engine of a car, a Jupyter notebook is the **diagnostic dashboard and laboratory** where you test how that engine performs under different conditions, with Claude acting as the technician who can read\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, 15464
[["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have shifted from using \\\"throwaway\\\" Jupyter notebooks to using Claude Code to build **permanent React dashboards** that can be reused for ongoing model evaluations and analytics [2, 3].\\n\\nIn essence, a Jupyter notebook in this context is a **collaborative workspace for data analysis** that Claude Code can help manage, document, and improve through natural language commands [1, 4].\\n\\nTo think of it another way: if your codebase is the engine of a car, a Jupyter notebook is the **diagnostic dashboard and laboratory** where you test how that engine performs under different conditions, with Claude acting as the technician who can read the gauges and tidy up the report.\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to \"]],[52,69,[\"Jupyter notebooks\",[true]]],[69,71,[\" (\"]],[71,77,[\".ipynb\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[77,192,[\" files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code.\"]]]]],[192,267,[[[192,267,[\"Claude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\"]]]]],[267,464,[[[267,295,[\"Interaction and Exploration:\",[true]]],[295,312,[\" Claude Code can \"]],[312,326,[\"read and write\",[true]]],[326,363,[\" Jupyter notebooks and is capable of \"]],[363,383,[\"interpreting outputs\",[true]]],[383,464,[\", including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly.\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"•\",1,1,0]]],[464,745,[[[464,485,[\"Aesthetic Refinement:\",[true]]],[485,529,[\" Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make \"]],[529,551,[\"aesthetic improvements\",[true]]],[551,745,[\" to a notebook before sharing it with others. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"•\",1,2,1]]],[745,871,[[[745,766,[\"Recommended \"]],[1036,1062,[\"permanent React dashboards\",[true]]],[1062,1126,[\" that can be reused for ongoing model evaluations and analytics.\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"•\",1,4,3]]],[1126,1310,[[[1126,1178,[\"In essence, a Jupyter notebook in this context is a \"]],[1178,1219,[\"collaborative workspace for data analysis\",[true]]],[1219,1310,[\" that Claude Code can help manage, document, and improve through natural language commands.\"]]]]],[1310,1593,[[[1310,1405,[\"To think of it another way: if your codebase is the engine of a car, a Jupyter notebook is the \"]],[1405,1440,[\"diagnostic dashboard and laboratory\",[true]]],[1440,1593,[\" where you test how that engine performs under different conditions, with Claude acting as the technician who can read the gauges and tidy up the report.\"]]]]]],[[[\"4406219b-f663-4120-b3ad-f982b09c92be\"],[null,78,191]],[[\"4406219b-f663-4120-b3ad-f982b09c92be\"],[null,383,463]],[[\"4406219b-f663-4120-b3ad-f982b09c92be\"],[null,552,595]],[[\"4406219b-f663-4120-b3ad-f982b09c92be\"],[null,595,744]],[[\"4406219b-f663-4120-b3ad-f982b09c92be\"],[null,848,870]],[[\"92c3503d-f782-498f-a043-dd2097f16b88\"],[null,1063,1125]],[[\"a24bacd9-769a-4fcb-b491-8c0c078a4894\"],[null,1063,1125]],[[\"4406219b-f663-4120-b3ad-f982b09c92be\"],[null,1220,1309]],[[\"24219d14-5e68-4f50-84bb-1a006c404c89\"],[null,1220,1309]]]],null,null,[[[\"4406219b-f663-4120-b3ad-f982b09c92be\"],[null,null,0.6795124788671918,[[null,15588,16370]],[[[15588,15681,[[[15588,15681,[\"This eliminates the need to remember  gh  command line syntax while automating routine tasks.\"]]],[null,1]]],[15681,15725,[[[15681,15725,[\"h. Use Claude to work with Jupyter notebooks\"]]],[null,6]]],[15725,16071,[[[15725,16071,[\"Researchers and data scientists at Anthropic use Claude Code to read and write Jupyter notebooks. Claude can interpret outputs, including images, providing a fast way to explore and interact with data. There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. Specifically telling it to make the notebook or its data visualizations “aesthetically pleasing” tends to help remind it that it’s optimizing for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"6ad4fced-a87f-42bc-a11f-b92cc700fa5f\"],\"c974f500-e788-4321-979e-0256bf205616\"]],[\"4406219b-f663-4120-b3ad-f982b09c92be\"]]],[[\"92c3503d-f782-498f-a043-dd2097f16b88\"],[null,null,0.6624827946303096,[[null,21021,21557]],[[[21021,21089,[[[21021,21089,[\"that’s too complex for editor macros but not large enough for major \"]]]]],[21089,21161,[[[21089,21161,[\"development effort, they use Claude Code like a “slot machine” - commit \"]]]]],[21161,21230,[[[21161,21230,[\"their state, let Claude work autonomously for 30 minutes, and either \"]]]]],[21230,21288,[[[21230,21288,[\"accept the solution or restart fresh if it doesn’t work.\\n \"]]]]],[21288,21356,[[[21288,21356,[\"Creating persistent analytics tools instead of throwaway notebooks\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21356,21426,[[[21356,21426,[\"Instead of building one-off Jupyter notebooks that get discarded, the \"]]]]],[21426,21491,[[[21426,21491,[\"team now has Claude build permanent React dashboards that can be \"]]]]],[21491,21557,[[[21491,21557,[\"reused across future model evaluations. This is important because \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"92c3503d-f782-498f-a043-dd2097f16b88\"]]],[[\"a24bacd9-769a-4fcb-b491-8c0c078a4894\"],[null,null,0.6728679597820794,[[null,21557,22083]],[[[21557,21629,[[[21557,21629,[\"understanding Claude’s performance is “one of the most important things \"]]]]],[21629,21695,[[[21629,21695,[\"for the team” - they need to understand how models perform during \"]]]]],[21695,21769,[[[21695,21769,[\"training and evaluations, which “is actually non-trivial and simple tools \"]]]]],[21769,21836,[[[21769,21836,[\"can’t get too much signal from looking at a single number go up.”\\n \"]]]]],[21836,21869,[[[21836,21869,[\"Zero-dependency task delegation\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21869,21942,[[[21869,21942,[\"For tasks in completely unfamiliar codebases or languages, they delegate \"]]]]],[21942,22013,[[[21942,22013,[\"entire implementation to Claude Code, leveraging its ability to gather \"]]]]],[22013,22083,[[[22013,22083,[\"context from the monorepo and execute tasks without their involvement \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"a24bacd9-769a-4fcb-b491-8c0c078a4894\"]]],[[\"24219d14-5e68-4f50-84bb-1a006c404c89\"],[null,null,0.6614029868224456,[[null,1033,1575]],[[[1033,1408,[[[1033,1097,[\"Get your team access to the full DataCamp for business platform.\",[null,null,null,\"\"]]],[1097,1408,[\"Claude Code  is an agentic coding tool developed by Anthropic that operates directly in the terminal, assisting developers in refactoring, documenting, and debugging code efficiently. By understanding the entire codebase, Claude Code helps simplify workflows, making it a powerful tool for software development.\"]]],[null,1]]],[1408,1575,[[[1408,1575,[\"In this tutorial, I’ll explain how to use Claude Code to improve software development workflows by refactoring, documenting, and debugging code. Specifically, we will:\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"62969650-0dff-481c-bba3-8d5fe63e9b31\"],\"cae7a337-fdf0-4a29-9afe-cea19ba0446f\"]],[\"24219d14-5e68-4f50-84bb-1a006c404c89\"]]]],1]],[[null,null,0.6795124788671918,[[null,15588,16370]],[[[15588,15681,[[[15588,15681,[\"This eliminates the need to remember  gh  command line syntax while automating routine tasks.\"]]],[null,1]]],[15681,15725,[[[15681,15725,[\"h. 15464
[["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have shifted from using \\\"throwaway\\\" Jupyter notebooks to using Claude Code to build **permanent React dashboards** that can be reused for ongoing model evaluations and analytics [2, 3].\\n\\nIn essence, a Jupyter notebook in this context is a **collaborative workspace for data analysis** that Claude Code can help manage, document, and improve through natural language commands [1, 4].\\n\\nTo think of it another way: if your codebase is the engine of a car, a Jupyter notebook is the **diagnostic dashboard and laboratory** where you test how that engine performs under different conditions, with Claude acting as the technician who can read the gauges and tidy up the report.\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code.\"]]]]],[192,267,[[[192,267,[\"Claude Code provides several capabilities for working with these There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. Specifically telling it to make the notebook or its data visualizations “aesthetically pleasing” tends to help remind it that it’s optimizing for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"6ad4fced-a87f-42bc-a11f-b92cc700fa5f\"],\"c974f500-e788-4321-979e-0256bf205616\"]],[\"784b8008-77ca-4099-ab34-d97cc5690c4c\"]]],[[\"8268f1d3-831b-49a7-bc1e-b7d0b5e7fb1d\"],[null,null,0.6624827946303096,[[null,21021,21557]],[[[21021,21089,[[[21021,21089,[\"that’s too complex for editor macros but not large enough for major \"]]]]],[21089,21161,[[[21089,21161,[\"development effort, they use Claude Code like a “slot machine” - commit \"]]]]],[21161,21230,[[[21161,21230,[\"their state, let Claude work autonomously for 30 minutes, and either \"]]]]],[21230,21288,[[[21230,21288,[\"accept the solution or restart fresh if it doesn’t work.\\n \"]]]]],[21288,21356,[[[21288,21356,[\"Creating persistent analytics tools instead of throwaway notebooks\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21356,21426,[[[21356,21426,[\"Instead of building one-off Jupyter notebooks that get discarded, the \"]]]]],[21426,21491,[[[21426,21491,[\"team now has Claude build permanent React dashboards that can be \"]]]]],[21491,21557,[[[21491,21557,[\"reused across future model evaluations. This is important because \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"8268f1d3-831b-49a7-bc1e-b7d0b5e7fb1d\"]]],[[\"81765ec8-be8d-4c50-808e-957c5ae0ebfa\"],[null,null,0.6728679597820794,[[null,21557,22083]],[[[21557,21629,[[[21557,21629,[\"understanding Claude’s performance is “one of the most important things \"]]]]],[21629,21695,[[[21629,21695,[\"for the team” - they need to understand how models perform during \"]]]]],[21695,21769,[[[21695,21769,[\"training and evaluations, which “is actually non-trivial and simple tools \"]]]]],[21769,21836,[[[21769,21836,[\"can’t get too much signal from looking at a single number go up.”\\n \"]]]]],[21836,21869,[[[21836,21869,[\"Zero-dependency task delegation\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21869,21942,[[[21869,21942,[\"For tasks in completely unfamiliar codebases or languages, they delegate \"]]]]],[21942,22013,[[[21942,22013,[\"entire implementation to Claude Code, leveraging its ability to gather \"]]]]],[22013,22083,[[[22013,22083,[\"context from the monorepo and execute tasks without their involvement \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"81765ec8-be8d-4c50-808e-957c5ae0ebfa\"]]],[[\"7c6a84ec-2c1f-4207-bcd3-01ed60ce9f6f\"],[null,null,0.6614029868224456,[[null,1033,1575]],[[[1033,1408,[[[1033,1097,[\"Get your team access to the full DataCamp for business platform.\",[null,null,null,\"\"]]],[1097,1408,[\"Claude Code  is an agentic coding tool developed by Anthropic that operates directly in the terminal, assisting developers in refactoring, documenting, and debugging code efficiently. By understanding the entire codebase, Claude Code helps simplify workflows, making it a powerful tool for software development.\"]]],[null,1]]],[1408,1575,[[[1408,1575,[\"In this tutorial, I’ll explain how to use Claude Code to improve software development workflows by refactoring, documenting, and debugging code. Specifically, we will:\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"62969650-0dff-481c-bba3-8d5fe63e9b31\"],\"cae7a337-fdf0-4a29-9afe-cea19ba0446f\"]],[\"7c6a84ec-2c1f-4207-bcd3-01ed60ce9f6f\"]]]],1]],[[null,null,0.6795124788671918,[[null,15588,16370]],[[[15588,15681,[[[15588,15681,[\"This eliminates the need to remember  gh  command line syntax while automating routine tasks.\"]]],[null,1]]],[15681,15725,[[[15681,15725,[\"h. Use Claude to work with Jupyter notebooks\"]]],[null,6]]],[15725,16071,[[[15725,16071,[\"Researchers and data scientists at Anthropic use Claude Code to read and write Jupyter notebooks. Claude can interpret outputs, including images, providing a fast way to explore and interact with data. There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. Specifically telling it to make the notebook or its data visualizations “aesthetically pleasing” tends to help remind it that it’s optimizing for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"6ad4fced-a87f-42bc-a11f-b92cc700fa5f\"],\"c974f500-e788-4321-979e-0256bf205616\"]],[\"784b8008-77ca-4099-ab34-d97cc5690c4c\"]],[null,null,0.6624827946303096,[[null,21021,21557]],[[[21021,21089,[[[21021,21089,[\"that’s too complex for editor macros but not large enough for major \"]]]]],[21089,21161,[[[21089,21161,[\"development effort, they use Claude Code like a “slot machine” - commit \"]]]]],[21161,21230,[[[21161,21230,[\"their state, let Claude work autonomously for 30 minutes, and either \"]]]]],[21230,21288,[[[21230,21288,[\"accept the solution or restart fresh if it doesn’t work.\\n \"]]]]],[21288,21356,[[[21288,21356,[\"Creating persistent analytics tools instead of throwaway notebooks\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21356,21426,[[[21356,21426,[\"Instead of building one-off Jupyter notebooks that get discarded, the \"]]]]],[21426,21491,[[[21426,21491,[\"team now has Claude build permanent React dashboards that can be \"]]]]],[21491,21557,[[[21491,21557,[\"reused across future model evaluations. This is important because \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"8268f1d3-831b-49a7-bc1e-b7d0b5e7fb1d\"]],[null,null,0.6728679597820794,[[null,21557,22083]],[[[21557,21629,[[[21557,21629,[\"understanding Claude’s performance is “one of the most important things \"]]]]],[21629,21695,[[[21629,21695,[\"for the team” - they need to understand how models perform during \"]]]]],[21695,21769,[[[21695,21769,[\"training and evaluations, which “is actually non-trivial and simple tools \"]]]]],[21769,21836,[[[21769,21836,[\"can’t get too much signal from looking at a single number go up.”\\n \"]]]]],[21836,21869,[[[21836,21869,[\"Zero-dependency task delegation\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21869,21942,[[[21869,21942,[\"For tasks in completely unfamiliar codebases or languages, they delegate \"]]]]],[21942,22013,[[[21942,22013,[\"entire implementation to Claude Code, leveraging its ability to gather \"]]]]],[22013,22083,[[[22013,22083,[\"context from the monorepo and execute tasks without their involvement \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"81765ec8-be8d-4c50-808e-957c5ae0ebfa\"]],[null,null,0.6614029868224456,[[null,1033,1575]],[[[1033,1408,[[[1033,1097,[\"Get your team access to the full DataCamp for business platform.\",[null,null,null,\"\"]]],[1097,1408,[\"Claude Code  is an agentic coding tool developed by Anthropic that operates directly in the terminal, assisting developers in refactoring, documenting, and debugging code efficiently. 15464
[["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have shifted from using \\\"throwaway\\\" Jupyter notebooks to using Claude Code to build **permanent React dashboards** that can be reused for ongoing model evaluations and analytics [2, 3].\\n\\nIn essence, a Jupyter notebook in this context is a **collaborative workspace for data analysis** that Claude Code can help manage, document, and improve through natural language commands [1, 4].\\n\\nTo think of it another way: if your codebase is the engine of a car, a Jupyter notebook is the **diagnostic dashboard and laboratory** where you test how that engine performs under different conditions, with Claude acting as the technician who can read the gauges and tidy up the report.\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code.\"]]]]],[192,267,[[[192,267,[\"Claude Code provides several capabilities for working with these There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a  .ipynb  file open side-by-side in VS Code.\"]]],[null,1]]],[16071,16370,[[[16071,16370,[\"You can also ask Claude to clean up or make aesthetic improvements to your Jupyter notebook before you show it to colleagues. Specifically telling it to make the notebook or its data visualizations “aesthetically pleasing” tends to help remind it that it’s optimizing for a human viewing experience.\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"6ad4fced-a87f-42bc-a11f-b92cc700fa5f\"],\"c974f500-e788-4321-979e-0256bf205616\"]],[\"784b8008-77ca-4099-ab34-d97cc5690c4c\"]],[null,null,0.6624827946303096,[[null,21021,21557]],[[[21021,21089,[[[21021,21089,[\"that’s too complex for editor macros but not large enough for major \"]]]]],[21089,21161,[[[21089,21161,[\"development effort, they use Claude Code like a “slot machine” - commit \"]]]]],[21161,21230,[[[21161,21230,[\"their state, let Claude work autonomously for 30 minutes, and either \"]]]]],[21230,21288,[[[21230,21288,[\"accept the solution or restart fresh if it doesn’t work.\\n \"]]]]],[21288,21356,[[[21288,21356,[\"Creating persistent analytics tools instead of throwaway notebooks\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21356,21426,[[[21356,21426,[\"Instead of building one-off Jupyter notebooks that get discarded, the \"]]]]],[21426,21491,[[[21426,21491,[\"team now has Claude build permanent React dashboards that can be \"]]]]],[21491,21557,[[[21491,21557,[\"reused across future model evaluations. This is important because \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"8268f1d3-831b-49a7-bc1e-b7d0b5e7fb1d\"]],[null,null,0.6728679597820794,[[null,21557,22083]],[[[21557,21629,[[[21557,21629,[\"understanding Claude’s performance is “one of the most important things \"]]]]],[21629,21695,[[[21629,21695,[\"for the team” - they need to understand how models perform during \"]]]]],[21695,21769,[[[21695,21769,[\"training and evaluations, which “is actually non-trivial and simple tools \"]]]]],[21769,21836,[[[21769,21836,[\"can’t get too much signal from looking at a single number go up.”\\n \"]]]]],[21836,21869,[[[21836,21869,[\"Zero-dependency task delegation\\u2028 \"]]]]],[21869,21942,[[[21869,21942,[\"For tasks in completely unfamiliar codebases or languages, they delegate \"]]]]],[21942,22013,[[[21942,22013,[\"entire implementation to Claude Code, leveraging its ability to gather \"]]]]],[22013,22083,[[[22013,22083,[\"context from the monorepo and execute tasks without their involvement \"]]]]]]],[[[\"bce3db69-fb2f-44bf-937c-fa9141c5d960\"],\"dc4fec86-0b72-4260-8325-fbe4be8aa7ed\"]],[\"81765ec8-be8d-4c50-808e-957c5ae0ebfa\"]],[null,null,0.6614029868224456,[[null,1033,1575]],[[[1033,1408,[[[1033,1097,[\"Get your team access to the full DataCamp for business platform.\",[null,null,null,\"\"]]],[1097,1408,[\"Claude Code  is an agentic coding tool developed by Anthropic that operates directly in the terminal, assisting developers in refactoring, documenting, and debugging code efficiently. By understanding the entire codebase, Claude Code helps simplify workflows, making it a powerful tool for software development.\"]]],[null,1]]],[1408,1575,[[[1408,1575,[\"In this tutorial, I’ll explain how to use Claude Code to improve software development workflows by refactoring, documenting, and debugging code. Specifically, we will:\"]]],[null,1]]]]],[[[\"62969650-0dff-481c-bba3-8d5fe63e9b31\"],\"cae7a337-fdf0-4a29-9afe-cea19ba0446f\"]],[\"7c6a84ec-2c1f-4207-bcd3-01ed60ce9f6f\"]]],[[[null,78,191],[0]],[[null,383,463],[0]],[[null,552,595],[0]],[[null,595,744],[0]],[[null,848,870],[0]],[[null,1063,1125],[1,2]],[[null,1220,1309],[0,3]]],null,false]"]]
15924
[["wrb.fr",null,"[[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to **Jupyter notebooks** (`.ipynb` files), which are frequently used by researchers and data scientists at Anthropic in conjunction with Claude Code [1].\\n\\nClaude Code provides several capabilities for working with these notebooks:\\n\\n*   **Interaction and Exploration:** Claude Code can **read and write** Jupyter notebooks and is capable of **interpreting outputs**, including images, which allows users to interact with and explore data quickly [1].\\n*   **Aesthetic Refinement:** Users can ask Claude to \\\"clean up\\\" or make **aesthetic improvements** to a notebook before sharing it with others [1]. Specifically instructing Claude to make visualizations \\\"aesthetically pleasing\\\" helps the model optimize the results for a human viewing experience [1].\\n*   **Recommended Workflow:** A common practice is to have **Claude Code and the notebook file open side-by-side** in an IDE like VS Code [1].\\n*   **Alternative for Persistence:** While notebooks are useful for research, some teams have shifted from using \\\"throwaway\\\" Jupyter notebooks to using Claude Code to build **permanent React dashboards** that can be reused for ongoing model evaluations and analytics [2, 3].\\n\\nIn essence, a Jupyter notebook in this context is a **collaborative workspace for data analysis** that Claude Code can help manage, document, and improve through natural language commands [1, 4].\\n\\nTo think of it another way: if your codebase is the engine of a car, a Jupyter notebook is the **diagnostic dashboard and laboratory** where you test how that engine performs under different conditions, with Claude acting as the technician who can read the gauges and tidy up the report.\",null,[\"f1fe8352-9971-4ec6-9991-5c291b68fa57\",\"3bf20740-ae68-435a-add8-a258be3708e0\",1027350177],null,[[[[0,192,[[[0,52,[\"Based on the sources, the term \\\"notebook\\\" refers to [["e",22,null,null,170191]]
